skill.name: ab-test-setup
skill.description: Plan, design, document, and interpret A/B tests with clear hypotheses, metrics, sample sizes, and rollout guidance.
skill.summary: Create statistically grounded A/B test plans and result summaries.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and hardcoded URL patterns. Review found the command, cryptography, and reconnaissance hits are false positives from Markdown fences, A/B testing language, and document templates. The only confirmed risk factor is low-risk reference links to public sample size calculators.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown Templates
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown code fences and A/B test document templates. No shell command, interpreter call, or user-controlled command construction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are fenced Markdown blocks and static templates. They do not contain executable shell syntax or any mechanism for command invocation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Calculator Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to public A/B test sample size calculators. These are documentation references, not active network requests or data exfiltration code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only confirmed network indicators are plain URLs in a resources section. No code path sends requests or transmits user data.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and reconnaissance matches are ordinary A/B testing terms, headings, and analysis prompts. No cryptographic API, host probing, scanning command, or data collection logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found instructional A/B testing content at these locations. I found no evidence of cryptographic operations, system reconnaissance, network probing, or prompt injection.

content.value_statement: Teams often run experiments without clear hypotheses, sample sizes, or success rules. This skill creates structured A/B test plans that improve decision quality.
actual_capabilities: Builds A/B test hypotheses using observation, change, expected outcome, audience, and metric.
Recommends test types, including A/B, A/B/n, multivariate, and split URL tests.
Selects primary, secondary, and guardrail metrics for common marketing and product tests.
Estimates sample size and duration using baseline rate, minimum detectable effect, power, and significance.
Creates test plan documents, result summaries, and next-step recommendations.
limitations: Does not run experiments or change traffic allocation inside testing platforms.
Does not replace a statistician for high-stakes or regulated experiments.
Requires baseline conversion rate, traffic, and business goals for precise planning.
Links to external calculators but does not verify their current availability.

use_cases.title: Landing Page Experiment Planning
use_cases.description: Create a clear test plan for headline, CTA, layout, or offer changes on a marketing page.

use_cases.title: Feature Rollout Measurement
use_cases.description: Define metrics, guardrails, and sample requirements before testing a product change.

use_cases.title: Experiment Review and Learning
use_cases.description: Summarize completed test results and convert outcomes into documented learnings.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Test Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single page change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an A/B test for this change: [describe change]. Baseline conversion is [rate], daily traffic is [traffic], and the goal is [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Choose Metrics and Guardrails
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate metric selection for a proposed experiment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the primary metric, secondary metrics, and guardrails for an A/B test of [change] on [page or feature].

prompt_templates.title: Estimate Sample Size and Duration
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning when baseline and lift targets are known.
prompt_templates.prompt: Estimate the sample size and duration for an A/B test with baseline conversion [rate], minimum detectable effect [effect], and daily eligible traffic [traffic].

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Completed Results
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review after a test has ended.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these A/B test results and recommend a decision. Include statistical significance, practical significance, guardrail risks, and follow-up tests: [results].

output_examples.input: Plan a test for changing a homepage CTA from 'Learn more' to 'Start free trial'.
output_examples.output: A structured plan with hypothesis, control, variant, primary CTA click metric, downstream signup guardrail, 50/50 allocation, and success criteria.

output_examples.input: We have a 3 percent baseline rate and want to detect a 20 percent lift.
output_examples.output: A sample size and duration estimate, plus guidance to run through complete business cycles before calling the result.

output_examples.input: Our variant improved clicks but reduced trial activation.
output_examples.output: A result interpretation that weighs primary gains against guardrail harm and recommends whether to ship, iterate, or retest.

best_practices: Provide baseline conversion rate, daily eligible traffic, target lift, and testing tool before requesting a plan.
Define one primary metric before launch and use guardrails to catch harmful side effects.
Document the hypothesis, variants, sample size, dates, decision rule, and final learning.
anti_patterns: Do not stop a test early only because interim results look favorable.
Do not test many unrelated changes when you need to isolate the cause.
Do not choose success metrics after seeing the results.

faq.question: Can this skill calculate exact sample sizes?
faq.answer: It can estimate requirements and guide inputs. Use a validated calculator or statistics package for final numbers.

faq.question: Does it support multivariate testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when multivariate testing is appropriate and warns about higher traffic requirements.

faq.question: Can it help choose between client-side and server-side testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares implementation approaches based on page type, performance sensitivity, and development complexity.

faq.question: Does it integrate with PostHog, Optimizely, or VWO?
faq.answer: It can plan tests for those tools, but it does not configure them directly.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Share the proposed change, baseline conversion rate, traffic volume, target audience, and smallest useful improvement.

faq.question: Can it review completed test results?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize outcomes, assess significance, check guardrails, and recommend next actions.
